address: Dechanuchit Road, Hua Hin, Prachuap Khiri Khan 77110
best_time_to_visit: Arrive around 18:00 when all stalls are fully set up but before the peak 20:00 rush

description.en:
## Hua Hin's Legendary Nightly Food Street

Hua Hin Night Market, centred on Dechanuchit Road in the heart of the old town, is a nightly food and shopping event that draws visitors and locals in equal measure. Open every evening from 17:00, the narrow street fills with dozens of stalls, restaurants, and bars creating one of the Gulf coast's liveliest night-time atmospheres.

## Seafood Heaven

Seafood is the star here. Vendors grill enormous tiger prawns over charcoal and serve them with spicy nam jim dipping sauce. Whole pla kapong (sea bass) is wrapped in lemongrass and grilled in banana leaf, while half-metre-long squid is charred until the edges caramelise. Most grilled seafood dishes cost between 120 and 300 THB depending on size, making it excellent value for the quality.

## Street Food Highlights

Beyond seafood, the market is packed with classic Thai street food. Look for hoi tod (crispy oyster or mussel pancakes), which is arguably Hua Hin's single most iconic street dish. Pad thai plates start at 80 THB, chicken skewers come three for 100 THB, and som tam (papaya salad) is around 60 THB. For dessert, grab mango sticky rice or watch a roti vendor prepare fresh pancakes with banana and chocolate.

## Shopping

Between the food stalls you will find vendors selling Thai silk scarves, elephant-print trousers, coconut oil products, and handmade jewellery. Prices are reasonable and light bargaining is acceptable for non-food items.

## Getting There

Dechanuchit Road connects to Phetkasem Road and is a short walk from Hua Hin Railway Station and the fishing pier. Parking is very limited, so walk or take a tuk-tuk.
description.nl: Bruisende nachtmarkt op Dechanuchit Road beroemd om verse zeevruchten, Thaise streetfood en souvenirs

tips: Eat at the open-air stalls rather than the sit-down restaurants for lower prices and more authentic food
Come hungry and graze — small portions from multiple stalls is the best strategy
The market gets very crowded after 20:00 on weekends so arrive early for a more relaxed experience
